Off Calendar Oct-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

ATTY. COLIN HARDACRE OF THE KAUFMAN LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. -310-286-2202 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JAY FINK's Motion To Strike Portions Of Defendant Byron Udell And Associates Inc.S Answer is Granted. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. JACOB HARKER, TEL. NO. 1-415-624-7602 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. COLIN HARDACRE OF THE KAUFMAN LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. -310-286-2202 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - ARGUED; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF JAY FINK DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT - Demurrer sustained, with ten days leave to amend, in part and overruled in part. Plaintiffs' demurrer to the second affirmative defense of laches is sustained, with ten days leave to amend, to give Defendant an opportunity to state facts to support its position that it has been prejudiced by Plaintiffs' alleged delay in filing the complaint. Plaintiffs' demurrer PLAINTIFF JAY FINK DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT - Demurrer sustained, with ten days leave to amend, in part and denied in part. Plaintiffs' demurrer to the second affirmative defense of laches is sustained, with ten days leave to amend, to give Defendant an opportunity to state facts to support its position that it has been prejudiced by Plaintiffs' alleged delay in filing the complaint. Plaintiffs' demurrer to the fourth affirmative defense of waiver, fifth affirmative defense of estoppel, sixth affirmative defense of unclean hands, ninth affirmative defense of unreasonable conduct, tenth affirmative defense of failure to take precautions, fourteenth affirmative defense of consent or acquiescence, and the fifteenth, sixteenth, and seventeenth affirmative defenses based on the unconstitutionality of a punitive damage award is sustained, with ten days leave to amend, to give Defendant an opportunity to allege facts which support its defenses. There are not facts in the complaint that support Defendant's defenses, thus, Defendant must state its own facts in the answer. (CCP section 431.30.) Plaintiffs' demurrer to the eighth affirmative defense of contribution, eleventh affirmative defense of intervening superseding causes, and the thirteenth affirmative defense of indemnity is sustained, without leave to amend. The defenses are inapplicable in Plaintiffs' suit against Defendant because Defendant is strictly liable for advertising in a commercial e-mail that violates the substantive provisions described in Business & Professions Code section 17529.5. (Hypertouch v. ValueClick Inc. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 805, 820-21.) Plaintiffs agree that if they were to enter into any settlement agreements with any other parties in the future, related to the instant spams, Defendant would be entitled to a corresponding reduction in damages. Thus, Plaintiffs' demurrer to the twelfth affirmative defense of reduction of damages is denied. Plaintiffs' demurrer to the eighteenth cause of action is overruled and nineteenth affirmative defenses is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Under, Business & Professions Code section 17206(d), only the Attorney General may recover disgorgement of profits. Plaintiffs' demurrer to the nineteenth affirmative defense of reduction in liquidated damages is sustained, with ten days leave to amend. In its answer Defendant parroted the statutory language without stating or referencing any facts to support its defense. Defendant agrees to withdraw affirmative defenses seven and twenty-one. As to the 20th affirmative defense is sustained without leave to amend awaiting for defense counsel's approval. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
